name: Ovenroasted Shiitake Mushrooms with Garlic and Coarse Salt
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon very finely minced or pureed fresh garlic
1 1/2 teaspoons coarse sea salt (if salt is in large crystals,
Herb sprigs – optional, for garnish (thyme or a bit of rosemary)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Spray the pan with oil spray.
3. Place Shiitake mushrooms in the baking dish; caps up or down—
they don't matter. Ensure there is enough space so they have air and can roast properly.
4. Toss mushrooms with olive oil, garlic, and salt.
5. Bake for 10 to 12 minutes.
6. Remove from oven and shake the pan a few times.
7. Check that some mushrooms are lightly golden on the lighter side
of their caps. Return them to the oven for another 3 to 5 minutes,
or longer if needed, as cooking time depends on initial heat.
8. Serve warm or at room temperature with an optional herb garnish.
Yield: 6–8 servings (serves as a starter; typically two portions)
